channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Exponential growth but for how long: DeepSeek's meteoric rise in AI-land continues unabated, fresh Similarweb data shows
item.description: Data from Similarweb shows DeepSeek is now the third most used AI tool, but is its popularity already waning?
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Sony PlayStation Network outage enrages gamers around the world
item.description: Sony’s PlayStation Network (PSN) is experiencing a major outage across the world, leaving tens of thousands of gamers unable to access online services, stores and multimedia apps.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump gives vote of confidence in Musk on DOGE
item.description: President Trump on Friday gave a vote of confidence in tech entrepreneur Elon Musk’s work on the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), saying that his billionaire ally is doing a “very good job.” “Elon is doing a great job. He’s finding tremendous fraud and corruption and waste.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Musk asks X if he should rehire DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk took to his social media platform X to ask users if he should rehire one of his deputies who resigned Thursday after racist social media posts of his resurfaced.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Can’t buy an Nvidia RTX 5080 or 5090 GPU due to stock shortages? If you were hoping GeForce Now might provide a good alternative, we’ve got some bad news
item.description: Nvidia GeForce Now goes from bad to worse for sign-ups, with no plans available at all, now – but day passes will return in a couple of weeks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Honeywell se transforma a partir de la IA generativa
item.description: Tras haber pasado años transformándose en una empresa totalmente basada en datos, el conglomerado estadounidense Honeywell está ahora preparado para aprovechar la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa en todas las facetas de su negocio para impulsar la mejora de la productividad, la colaboración y
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows PowerToys: Your handy productivity toolbox
item.description: Back in the 1990s, Windows power users often availed themselves of a set of small, free software tools from Microsoft collectively known as “PowerToys.” (Read about their history at Wikipedia.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Like TikTok, lawmakers want to ban DeepSeek from government devices
item.description: A bipartisan duo in the the U.S. House is proposing legislation to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek from federal devices, similar to the policy already in place for the popular social media platform TikTok. Reps. Josh Gottheimer, D-N.J., and Darin LaHood, R-Ill.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: La falta de formación no merma la concepción estratégica de la IA
item.description: Con el punto y final de un 2024 marcado por la irrupción popular de la inteligencia artificial (IA), se podría decir que este nuevo año supondrá la consagración de la tecnología en el plano político, empresarial y social.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Spy vs spy: Security agencies help secure the network edge
item.description: The national intelligence services of five countries have offered enterprises advice on beating spies at their own game in a series of documents intended to help them protect network edge devices and appliances such as firewalls, routers, VPN (virtual private networks) gateways, internet of things (
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Legislators demand truth about OPM email server
item.description: Two members of the US House of Representatives want answers from the US Office of Personnel Management (OPM) over allegations that a “server of unknown nature” was used last month to “access sensitive government data without regard for crucial security and privacy protections.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Malicious Typosquat Package in Go Language Ecosystem
item.description: A malicious typosquat package has been found in the Go language ecosystem, containing a backdoor to enable remote code execution.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to minimize your digital footprint when you travel
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips to dramatically reduce the risk of having your information stolen while booking flights, cruises, vehicles and accommodations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI-Powered Policing: The Future of Traffic Safety in Kazakhstan
item.description: Traffic management is a growing challenge for cities worldwide, requiring a balance between enforcement, efficiency, and public trust. In Kazakhstan, the Qorgau system is redefining road safety through an innovative fusion of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and mobile technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Qorgau: How Kazakhstan is Using AI to Make Roads Safer
item.description: Traffic management is a growing challenge for cities worldwide, requiring a balance between enforcement, efficiency, and public trust. In Kazakhstan, the Qorgau system is redefining road safety through an innovative fusion of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and mobile technology.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AWS tightens default security on Redshift
item.description: Amazon’s security improvements for its AWS Redshift managed data warehouse service are welcome additions, says an expert.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Automated builds, tests, and quality gates are key to software quality – report
item.description: When it comes to software quality, the top three practices cited for production readiness include automated building and deploying, automated tests, and automated quality gates for every merge request, according to the JetBrains Qodana State of Software Quality Report 2024.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hacked on social? Here’s what to do next
item.description: Spambots, data thieves, and other bad actors run rampant online — more so now than ever before. Here's what to do if one gets ahold of you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look inside the Trump administration approach to artificial intelligence
item.description: "I think we think of AI as primarily a bunch of the software geeks who are out there coding somewhere, but there's real hardware involved," said Neil Chilson. The post A look inside the Trump administration approach to artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: I’m a tech expert: 10 AI prompts you’ll use all the time
item.description: From proofreading and research to brainstorming and data analysis, you can use generative AI as a sidekick for just about any task you can dream of.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CISA hires former DHS CIO into top cyber position
item.description: Karen Evans is returning to the Department of Homeland Security in a top cyber job as DHS responds to major hacks into U.S. critical infrastructure. The post CISA hires former DHS CIO into top cyber position first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Big rigs deliver cargo with no humans at the wheel
item.description: First driverless semi delivers 100 loads in Texas, transforming logistics with AI. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his takeaways.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
